name: slopstop
description: Per-ticket tracking for Linear, JIRA, and GitHub Issues, with deep planning, PR creation, and ship-it commands. Sixteen slash commands — /slopstop:start, :plan, :update, :archive, :pr, :merge, :doc-sync, :create-gh, :gh-init, :update-ticket, :document, :grill, :design, :tickets, :focus, and :run — keep a durable per-ticket plan + findings + progress log outside the repo; grill a plan into shared understanding before ticket breakdown (:grill); run Stage 1 of the three-tier process — PRD + feature charter into the run dir, stopping at gate G1 (:design); cut the adversary-approved ticket tree from the PRD, stopping at gate G2 (:tickets); orchestrate the fleet against the approved tree — hermetic worktree agents, autonomous monitoring, adversarial handoff verification, serial integration — stopping at gate G-final (:run); re-point attribution mid-session without branch or ticket-system transition (:focus); investigate the codebase and produce a parallelism-aware plan with optional subagent fanout in git worktrees; open a PR with a pre-commit simplify pass, adversary gap-finder, slop-detection gate, project tests, and CodeRabbit or Claude review; wrap merge-PR + advance-one-workflow-state + document into one confirmed step; bootstrap GitHub projects in one command (:gh-init); and mirror design/ to the repo wiki or Linear Docs via :doc-sync.
